title: Database Breach
module_title: SQL Injection Exploitation and Database Extraction
learning_objectives: Identify SQL injection vulnerabilities through manual testing
Execute manual SQLi exploitation to extract database information
Deploy sqlmap for automated comprehensive database extraction
Assess WAF bypass techniques for injection payloads
Demonstrate business impact of database access
key_topics: SQL injection types: Union, Error-based, Blind, Time-based
scenario: A banking application contains SQL injection. Find it, exploit it manually and with sqlmap, extract data, and write the board-level report.

scenario_context.setting: Banking/financial application with sensitive customer data
scenario_context.challenge: Exploit SQLi to demonstrate data breach capability and compliance violation
scenario_context.relevance: Financial sector heavily regulated; demonstrates GDPR/PCI-DSS implications

subtasks.title: Vulnerability Discovery
subtasks.description: Manually test all inputs for SQLi.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Test all input fields (login, search, filters) with SQLi test strings. Identify injection points. Document injection type (Union, Error, Blind, Time-based).
subtasks.evidence_required: Injection point documentation with type and evidence

subtasks.title: Manual Exploitation
subtasks.description: Exploit without sqlmap. Extract DB version, names, tables.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Craft manual SQL injection payloads. Extract database version, list databases, enumerate tables and columns. Document each query and result.
subtasks.evidence_required: Manual exploitation log with successful queries

subtasks.title: Automated Exploitation
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Deploy sqlmap with appropriate options. Extract full database schema. Dump table data. Document sqlmap commands and results.

subtasks.title: WAF Bypass Attempt
subtasks.detailed_guidance: If WAF blocks requests, test: case variations, encoding, comments, multiple payloads. Document success rate per technique.
subtasks.evidence_required: WAF bypass log with techniques and results

subtasks.title: Impact Assessment
subtasks.description: What could an attacker do with this access?
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Assess data exposed (PII, financial, credentials). Calculate potential breach cost. Identify compliance violations (GDPR, PCI-DSS). Quantify business risk.
subtasks.evidence_required: Impact assessment with business risk quantification

subtasks.title: Professional Report
subtasks.description: Complete finding report per OCH template.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Write executive summary, technical details, CVSS score, remediation steps, compliance implications. Use professional formatting.

reflection_prompt: How did your manual exploitation approach differ from sqlmap's automated approach? What data is most critical to prioritize in a real breach scenario?

quiz.question: When should you choose Error-based SQL injection over Union-based?
options: When error messages reveal database info
quiz.explanation: Error-based SQLi is useful when error messages are verbose and reveal database structure. Union-based requires knowing column count and types.

quiz.question: What is sqlmap's primary advantage over manual exploitation?
quiz.explanation: sqlmap automates the enumeration and extraction process, handling different DBMS types and using multiple techniques automatically.

portfolio_artifacts: sqlmap execution output and data dump
mentor_notes: Assess balance between manual understanding and tool usage. Impact assessment must demonstrate business/compliance understanding. 72-hour deadline is tight; check time management and prioritization.
